comments.author: @hariprasads9971
comments.text: As long as they can’t let so called civilised peoples inside their territory they remain nature’s child rather than religion’s…

comments.author: @TerraVista-4K
comments.text: The comparison to the 'Zoo Hypothesis' at the end genuinely gave me goosebumps. It really makes you appreciate their zero-plastic, ecosystem-friendly lifestyle that has endured for 60,000 years. Sometimes, civilization isn't about assimilating everyone, but learning to leave them in peace.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you for such a thoughtful comment. That ending was meant to make people slow down and think, so I’m really glad it stayed with you. What part of their way of life challenged your view of “civilization” the most?

comments.author: @ChanelNatureTravel4K
comments.text: Few places on Earth remain as isolated from the modern world as North Sentinel Island. Surrounded by the waters of the Bay of Bengal, the island represents a rare example of a society that has continued to exist largely on its own terms, separated from the rapid changes that have transformed most of the planet.

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: Would you visit North Sentinel Island if it were completely safe, or do you think it should stay untouched forever?

comments.text: I am totally blown away by this tribe « No civilization— no problem “ I wonder why they haven’t left them a ship with young coconut 🌴 🥥 and banana 🍌 tress and a team of women gardeners to show them how to plant 🌱 those coconut 🌴 🥥 And bananas trees 🌲 on their island and other fruit plants. They would at least have their own bananas .I am sure someone out there thought of it before but why they haven’t done it. Thats a mystery…
